fix: Cleaned and Optimized S-Console Demo HTML for Better Readability and …
prs.body:
## 🧹 Summary
This PR improves the structure, readability, and consistency of the **S-Console demo HTML file**.   All unnecessary whitespace, inconsistent indentation, and redundant classes were cleaned while preserving functionality and styling.

---

## 🔧 Changes Made
- Reformatted the entire HTML for consistent indentation and readability.
- Organized sections (Header, Main, Footer) with clear comments.
- Removed redundant classes and unnecessary blank lines.
- Improved accessibility and semantic structure.
- Cleaned up meta tags, script imports, and Tailwind utility order.
- Ensured full responsiveness and minimal visual changes.

---

## ✅ Tested
- Verified layout consistency on desktop and mobile.
- All links, buttons, and console functionality working as expected.

prs.title: Added WordMaze Game
prs.body:
🎮 Added a new Python project: WordMaze

This game uses the Trie (Prefix Tree) data structure to efficiently validate word guesses. Players have 6 attempts to guess a hidden word, with feedback provided for each letter: 🟩 Correct letter and position  
🟨 Correct letter, wrong position  
⬜ Incorrect letter

📂 Includes:
- wordmaze.py (main game logic)
- words.txt (sample word list)
- README.md (project documentation)

💡 Concepts Used:
- Trie (Prefix Tree)
- String Matching Algorithm
- Random Selection

Perfect beginner-friendly DSA project for Hacktoberfest 2025!

prs.title: Create recipe_organizer.py
prs.body:
# Recipe Organizer CLI

A small CLI utility to manage recipe JSON samples (list, view, validate, search, add, export).

## Why this is useful
- Quickly validate JSON samples before a PR
- Search recipes by ingredients/tags/cuisine
- Combine multiple sample files into a single JSON for testing
- Add new samples interactively

## Requirements
- Python 3.7+

No external packages required.

## Installation
1. Fork the repo
2. Copy this folder to `python/recipe_organizer/` in your fork

## Usage
```bash
# list recipes in default folder data/samples
python recipe_organizer.py list --dir data/samples

# view a specific recipe
python recipe_organizer.py view data/samples/masala_chai.json

# validate files or folder
python recipe_organizer.py validate data/samples
python recipe_organizer.py validate data/samples/masala_chai.json

# search
python recipe_organizer.py search --ingredient "cardamom" --dir data/samples
python recipe_organizer.py search --tag "beverage" --dir data/samples
python recipe_organizer.py search --cuisine "Indian" --dir data/samples

# interactively add a new recipe
python recipe_organizer.py add --out data/samples

# export combined json
python recipe_organizer.py export --dir data/samples --out combined_recipes.json

prs.title: Create south_indian_filter_coffee.json
prs.body:
## ☕ What this PR adds
This PR adds a new JSON sample file — `south_indian_filter_coffee.json` — under `data/samples/`.

## 📋 Details
- Contains structured data for South Indian Filter Coffee
- Includes metadata such as cuisine, difficulty, prep time, nutrition facts, etc.
- Follows the same schema as `masala_chai.json`
- Ready for integration with Java-based data parsers or APIs

prs.title: Create SimpleApp.java
prs.body:
Added a simple Java console application for user interaction,

## 🎯 What this PR does
This pull request adds a simple Java console application (`SimpleApp.java`) that demonstrates:
- Taking user input using Scanner
- Displaying personalized output
- Using basic conditional logic

## 🚀 Why this change is important
This program is beginner-friendly and serves as an example for new Java developers participating in Hacktoberfest to understand:
- How to compile and run a Java program
- Basic syntax and input/output handling
- Simple decision-making with if-else

## 🧩 How to test
1. Compile the file:
   ```bash
   javac SimpleApp.java

prs.title: Simple Calculator
prs.body:
This PR adds a beautiful and responsive calculator web app built using HTML, TailwindCSS, and JavaScript.
It performs basic arithmetic operations with an elegant gradient red–peach theme, smooth animations, and a fully responsive layout.

✨ Features Added

🧮 Fully functional calculator (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide)
🎨 Aesthetic UI with light gradient theme
⚡ Smooth button animations and hover effects
📱 Responsive layout (mobile-friendly)
💡 Well-structured and readable code
📘 Added a detailed README.md for documentation

🧰 Tech Stack

HTML5
Tailwind CSS (via CDN)
Vanilla JavaScript (ES6)

prs.title: Create todo.py
prs.body:
# Problem
The repository lacks a simple, beginner-friendly Python example suitable for Hacktoberfest contributions.

# Solution
Add a minimal To-Do CLI app (`todo.py`) that saves tasks to `tasks.json` and a README with usage instructions.

## Changes Proposed
- Add `todo.py` (no external dependencies)
- Add `README-HACKTOBERFEST.md` with setup and commands
- Basic validation for empty titles and invalid indices

## Other Notes
- Tested locally with Python 3.7+
- Safe, self-contained addition that won’t affect existing code
